content.Text:
[Tue 2026-07-14 16:34 EDT] ## Planning Discipline
1. Read every file you will modify. Understand existing patterns first.
2. Before writing code, state your plan: what, why, which files, test case, risk.
3. When ambiguous, prefer: completeness over shortcuts, existing patterns over new ones, reversible choices over irreversible ones, safe defaults over clever ones.
4. Self-review your changes before reporting done. Check for: missed files, broken imports, untested paths, style inconsistencies.
5. Report when done: what shipped, what decisions you made, anything uncertain.

---

## Task: Add Google Sign-In to the Mobile App

### Context
The web app (`apps/web/app/signin/signin-form.tsx`) has "Continue with Google" using `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({provider: "google"})`. The mobile app (`apps/mobile/src/app/(auth)/signin.tsx`) only has email+password.

Google OAuth is already configured in the Supabase project (it works on web). The mobile app has:
- Expo SDK 56 with `expo-router`
- `expo-linking` already installed
- App scheme: `homa` (in app.json)
- Supabase client in `apps/mobile/src/lib/supabase.ts`

### What needs to happen
1. Add `expo-web-browser` package (needed for OAuth flows in Expo)
2. Update `apps/mobile/src/app/(auth)/signin.tsx` to add a "Continue with Google" button matching the web's visual pattern
3. The OAuth flow for React Native + Supabase uses `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth` with a redirect URL using the app scheme (`homa://`), and `expo-web-browser` to handle the browser session
4. Document that `homa://` (or the full redirect URL like `homa://google-auth`) needs to be added to the Supabase redirect allowlist (dashboard → Authentication → URL Configuration)

### Implementation approach (Supabase + Expo recommended pattern)
The standard pattern:
1. Call `supabase.auth.signInWithOAuth({provider: "google", options: {redirectTo: "homa://google-auth", skipBrowserRedirect: true}})` to get the OAuth URL
2. Open the URL with `expo-web-browser`'s `openAuthSessionAsync(url, redirectUrl)`
3. Parse the returned URL for the session tokens
4. Call `supabase.auth.setSession()` with the tokens

Reference: https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth/social-login/auth-google?queryGroups=platform&platform=react-native

### Visual requirements
- Match the web's button style: full-width, Google logo glyph on the left, "Continue with Google" text
- Place above the email/password fields with an "or" divider between them (same as web)
- Use the existing design tokens from `apps/mobile/src/theme/tokens.ts`
- Check the web's `GoogleGlyph` component at `apps/web/components/brand/GoogleGlyph.tsx` — create a React Native SVG equivalent for mobile (the app has `react-native-svg` installed)

### Important notes
- The mobile app uses `expo-router` with file-based routing
- Check if any deep link handling is needed in `apps/mobile/src/app/_layout.tsx`
- The SessionProvider at `apps/mobile/src/features/auth/session-provider.tsx` should automatically pick up the session from `onAuthStateChange`
- Document any Supabase dashboard config changes needed (redirect URLs) in a code comment
- Run `pnpm typecheck` from repo root to verify no type errors
- Push to a branch, open a PR to main
